commands.review_summary:
The branch adds HTML document detection and renders complete assistant or expanded tool-result HTML as a sandboxed iframe preview with source disclosure, plus tests, styles, and a changelog line.

PR surface: Source +240, Tests +344, Docs +1. Total +585 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR. The PR body and gist provide a before/after browser probe, and current main has no HTML preview implementation.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blockers are concrete file-level repairs: harden the CSP and tests, handle the fenced HTML edge case, and drop the changelog line.

target.title: [codex] feat(ui): render HTML assistant responses in sandboxed iframe
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: complete HTML documents returned by an assistant showed up as raw chat text, which made visual HTML output hard to inspect in Control UI.
- Why it matters: agents often produce standalone HTML reports or demos; users need a safe rendered preview without losing access to source.
- What changed: complete non-streaming assistant HTML responses now render as scriptless sandboxed iframe previews with collapsible source; expanded tool-result HTML gets the same preview path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): snippets, user/system/developer messages, streaming messages, collapsed tool outputs, JSON rendering, markdown rendering, and existing canvas/embed behavior stay on their prior paths.

AI-assisted by Codex GPT-5.5. Credit: Codex GPT-5.5.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A.

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: complete HTML documents preview only for non-streaming assistant messages and expanded tool outputs; snippets, user/system/developer, streaming, and collapsed tool outputs do not preview.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: detector unit tests cover HTML-shape decisions; grouped-render tests cover role/stream/tool rendering gates.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI chat now renders complete assistant HTML documents as sandboxed previews with a source disclosure instead of showing the whole document inline as markdown text. Long previews are capped and labeled as truncated.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
assistant complete HTML -> markdown text bubble -> raw <html> visible

After:
assistant complete HTML -> scriptless sandboxed iframe -> source disclosure available
expanded tool HTML -> scriptless sandboxed iframe -> source disclosure available
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security-relevant implementation details:

- The preview iframe uses an empty `sandbox` attribute, so scripts, same-origin access, forms, popups, and top navigation remain disabled.
- The iframe includes a restrictive `csp` attribute: `default-src 'none'; img-src data: blob:; media-src data: blob:; font-src data:; style-src 'unsafe-inline'`.
- Preview detection intentionally excludes snippets/prose and non-assistant roles.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local dev host
- Runtime/container: Node 24.14.1, pnpm, Vite dev server, Playwright Chromium
- Model/provider: N/A for runtime behavior; implementation/review assisted by Codex GPT-5.5
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI chat
- Relevant config (redacted): mocked Gateway WebSocket for browser probe

### Steps

1. Open Control UI chat.
2. Seed an assistant message whose content is a complete HTML document.
3. Compare `origin/main` and this branch.

### Expected

- `origin/main`: complete HTML appears as raw chat text.
- This branch: complete HTML appears in one sandboxed iframe preview; source remains available under `Source`.

### Actual

Matches expected in the browser probe.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Screenshot artifacts:

- Before/after screenshot gist: https://gist.github.com/wolph/207291436a424d13572b8964821f982e
- Before probe: `previewCount: 0`, `chatTextCount: 1`, `rawHtmlVisible: true`
- After probe: `previewCount: 1`, `chatTextCount: 0`, `rawHtmlVisible: false`, `sandbox: ""`, expected CSP present

Validation run locally:

- `pnpm build` passed.
- `pnpm check` passed.
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` passed: 57 tests.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.ts ui/src/ui/chat/html-preview.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/styles/chat/text.css` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- Browser probe passed against Vite dev servers for `origin/main` and this branch.
- `codex review --base origin/main` ran with Codex GPT-5.5. It returned findings against MCP Apps files (`src/agents/mcp-ui-resource.ts`, `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/mcp-app-proxy.ts`) that are not present in this branch diff; verified with `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD`.

Full-suite note:

- `pnpm test` was attempted and exited 1 due failures outside this branch's diff:
  - `src/gateway/server.canvas-auth.test.ts`: local host cannot bind IPv6 loopback `::1` (`EADDRNOTAVAIL`), confirmed with a direct Node `net.Server.listen(0, "::1")` probe.
  - `test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts`: local `/tmp/node-compile-cache/v24.14.1-x64-cf738c9d-0` is owned by `root`, causing an unrelated compile-cache prune warning assertion.
  - `src/logging/diagnostic-stability-bundle.test.ts` and `src/logging/diagnostic-support-export.test.ts`: this host's hostname is literally `linux`, so assertions that output does not contain `os.hostname()` collide with legitimate platform metadata containing `"linux"`.
- None of those failing files are in `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD` for this PR.

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: assistant complete HTML preview, snippets stay markdown, streaming stays markdown, user/system/developer stay markdown, expanded tool-result previews, collapsed tool output stays hidden.
- Edge cases checked: CRLF fenced HTML documents, htm/html fences, non-whole-message fences, paired head/body documents, truncation boundary, scriptless sandbox policy.
- What I did **not** verify: a fully green local `pnpm test` run, due the unrelated local-environment failures listed above.

Testing degree: broadly tested with documented unrelated local full-suite blockers; touched UI surface and repo build/check gates pass locally.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No PR bot conversations exist yet.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: accidentally previewing user/system/developer or partial HTML content.
  - Mitigation: detector and grouped-render tests cover role gates, streaming gates, tool expansion gates, snippets, prose, and incomplete documents.
- Risk: rendered HTML could execute script or reach out broadly.
  - Mitigation: iframe uses empty sandbox plus restrictive CSP, and no new network or command execution surface is added.
